subsections.title: Citation and Commencement
subsections.requirements: The CIIO must comply with the citation and commencement provisions of the Cybersecurity Act 2018.

subsections.title: Glossary and Interpretation
subsections.requirements: All terms used must align with the glossary and definitions as defined in the CCoP.

subsections.title: Purpose of this Code
subsections.requirements: The plan must demonstrate awareness of and alignment to the purpose of the CCoP.

subsections.title: Legal Effect of this Code
subsections.requirements: The plan must acknowledge the mandatory legal effect of this Code on Critical Information Infrastructure Owners (CIIOs).

subsections.title: Recurring Requirements
subsections.requirements: Recurring requirements such as annual reviews, periodic audits, and continuous monitoring must be defined.

subsections.title: Waiver
subsections.requirements: The plan must outline the process for obtaining and documenting waivers where applicable.

subsections.title: Amendment and Revocation
subsections.requirements: The plan must address how amendments and revocations of this Code will be tracked and communicated internally.

subsections.title: Remediation of Audit Findings
subsections.requirements: The CIIO shall establish a documented process to remediate findings from cybersecurity audits in a timely manner. Findings must be tracked, assigned owners, and closed within defined timelines.

subsections.title: Leadership and Oversight
subsections.requirements: The CIIO shall establish a cybersecurity governance structure with defined leadership roles, such as a Cybersecurity Steering Committee, CISO, and appointed officers. Senior management must be actively involved in cybersecurity decision-making.

subsections.title: Risk Management
subsections.requirements: The CIIO shall implement a risk management framework that includes regular risk assessments, risk treatment plans, and risk acceptance processes aligned to the organisation's risk appetite.

subsections.title: Policies, Standards, Guidelines and Procedures
subsections.requirements: Documented and approved cybersecurity policies, standards, guidelines, and procedures must exist. They must be reviewed and updated at least annually.

subsections.title: Security-by-Design
subsections.requirements: Security must be incorporated into the design and architecture of systems from the outset, including threat modelling and design reviews.

subsections.title: Cybersecurity Design Principles
subsections.requirements: Systems must be designed according to cybersecurity principles such as least privilege, defence-in-depth, zero trust, and separation of duties.

subsections.title: Change Management
subsections.requirements: A formal change management process must be in place covering impact assessment, approval workflows, and rollback procedures for changes to CII assets.

subsections.title: Use of Cloud Computing Systems and Services
subsections.requirements: The CIIO must assess and manage security risks when using cloud services, including shared responsibility models, data residency, and access controls.

subsections.title: Outsourcing and Vendor Management
subsections.requirements: Third-party and vendor risks must be managed through vendor assessments, contracts with security clauses, and ongoing monitoring of vendor compliance.

subsections.title: Asset Management
subsections.requirements: The CIIO shall establish and maintain a comprehensive inventory of all CII assets including hardware, software, dependencies, ownership, physical location, outsourced providers, and cloud services. The inventory must be kept up-to-date and include network topology diagrams.

subsections.title: Access Control
subsections.requirements: Access to CII must be restricted to authorised personnel. Authentication and authorisation controls commensurate with the cybersecurity risk profile must be implemented. Vendor access must be documented, approved, supervised, and performed on-site.

subsections.title: Account Management
subsections.requirements: User accounts must be managed throughout their lifecycle including creation, modification, review, and deletion. Dormant and shared accounts must be identified and controlled.

subsections.title: Privileged Access Management
subsections.requirements: Privileged accounts must have MFA, be granted on a need-to-use basis, be inventoried, and accessed only from hardened environments. Privilege escalation must be controlled.

subsections.title: Domain Controller
subsections.requirements: Domain controllers must be hardened, monitored, and protected with strict access controls. Replication and authentication traffic must be secured.

subsections.title: Network Segmentation
subsections.requirements: CII networks must be segmented into zones based on security levels. Communications between zones must be controlled and monitored.

subsections.title: Network Security
subsections.requirements: Firewalls, IDS/IPS, and other network security controls must be deployed and maintained. Network traffic must be monitored for anomalies.

subsections.title: Remote Connection
subsections.requirements: Remote access must be secured through VPN or equivalent encrypted channels with MFA. Remote sessions must be monitored and logged.

subsections.title: Wireless Communication
subsections.requirements: Wireless networks must be secured with appropriate encryption (WPA3 or equivalent), access controls, and monitoring.

subsections.title: System Hardening
subsections.requirements: All CII assets must be hardened by disabling unnecessary services, applying secure configurations, and maintaining hardening baselines.

subsections.title: Patch Management
subsections.requirements: Vulnerability patches must be applied within defined timelines based on severity. A patch management process must track, test, and deploy patches.

subsections.title: Portable Computing Devices and Removable Storage Media
subsections.requirements: Use of portable devices and removable storage must be controlled, encrypted, and logged. Unauthorised devices must be blocked.

subsections.title: Application Security
subsections.requirements: Secure development lifecycle (SDLC), code reviews, and application security testing must be implemented for CII applications.

subsections.title: Database Security
subsections.requirements: Databases must be hardened, access-controlled, encrypted, and regularly audited. Database activity must be logged and monitored.

subsections.title: Vulnerability Assessment
subsections.requirements: Regular vulnerability assessments must be conducted on CII assets. Findings must be tracked and remediated based on risk priority.

subsections.title: Penetration Testing
subsections.requirements: Penetration tests must be conducted at least annually by qualified testers. Results must be documented, reported to management, and remediated.

subsections.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation
subsections.requirements: Red team or adversarial exercises must be conducted to simulate realistic attack scenarios against CII.

subsections.title: Cryptographic Key Management
subsections.requirements: Cryptographic keys must be generated, stored, distributed, and destroyed using documented and secure procedures.

subsections.title: Logging
subsections.requirements: Comprehensive logging must be enabled across all CII assets. Logs must be centralised, protected from tampering, and retained for a minimum defined period.

subsections.title: Monitoring and Detection
subsections.requirements: Continuous monitoring for cybersecurity threats must be implemented using SIEM or equivalent tools. Alerting thresholds and escalation procedures must be defined.

subsections.title: Threat Hunting
subsections.requirements: Proactive threat hunting activities must be conducted to detect threats that evade automated detection tools.

subsections.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence and Information Sharing
subsections.requirements: The CIIO shall subscribe to and consume relevant cyber threat intelligence and participate in information sharing with sector peers and regulators.

subsections.title: Incident Management
subsections.requirements: A Cybersecurity Incident Response Plan (CIRP) must be established with defined CIRT structure, roles, reporting structure, communication protocols, and activation thresholds.

subsections.title: Crisis Communication Plan
subsections.requirements: A crisis communication plan must exist detailing how the CIIO will communicate with internal and external stakeholders during a cybersecurity incident.

subsections.title: Cybersecurity Exercise
subsections.requirements: Scenario-based cybersecurity exercises must be conducted at least annually to validate the CIRP and identify improvements.

subsections.title: Backup and Restoration Plan
subsections.requirements: Regular backups of CII data must be maintained, tested, and stored securely. Restoration procedures must be documented and verified.

subsections.title: Business Continuity Plan and Disaster Recovery Plan
subsections.requirements: A BCP and DRP must be established, tested at least annually, and updated to reflect changes to the CII.

subsections.title: Cybersecurity Awareness Programme
subsections.requirements: All staff must undergo a cybersecurity awareness programme covering threats, safe practices, and their responsibilities. The programme must be conducted regularly.

subsections.title: Cybersecurity Training and Skills
subsections.requirements: Technical staff and security personnel must receive role-specific cybersecurity training. Training records must be maintained.

subsections.title: Application of this Section
subsections.requirements: This section applies to CIIOs that operate OT systems. Applicability must be documented and justified.

subsections.title: OT Architecture and Security
subsections.requirements: OT systems must be segregated from IT networks. OT-specific security controls must be implemented and documented.

subsections.title: Secure Coding
subsections.requirements: Secure coding practices must be followed for any software developed for or integrated with OT systems.

subsections.title: Field Controllers
subsections.requirements: Field controllers and PLCs must be secured with access controls, firmware updates, and monitoring.

subsections.title: Application of this Section
subsections.requirements: Domain-specific practices apply where the CIIO operates within a specific regulated sector (e.g. finance, energy, water). Applicability must be documented.

subsections.title: Domain Name System Security Extensions (DNSSEC)
subsections.requirements: CIIOs that operate DNS infrastructure must implement DNSSEC to ensure integrity and authenticity of DNS responses.
